#  Delete an RFI 
##  Background 
When you delete an RFI, the system moves it to the Recycle Bin tab. By design, the Recycle Bin cannot be emptied. This ensures that project teams have the ability to restore deleted RFIs at any time throughout the course of the project.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * **Prerequisite** :
    * [Create an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/create-an-rfi)
  * **Additional Information** :
    * You can delete RFIs in the 'Draft', 'Open', or 'Closed' status, whether or not anyone has replied to the RFI or marked a reply as the official response.
    * When you delete an RFI, it is moved to the Recycle Bin in the RFIs tool.
    * To give project teams the ability to restore any deleted item, the Recycle Bin **cannot** be emptied. See [Retrieve an RFI from the Recycle Bin](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/retrieve-an-rfi-from-the-recycle-bin).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Locate the RFI that you want to send to delete.
  3. Click **View**.
  4. Click the vertical ellipsis![Icon Ellipsis Vertical](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/b6d0fa56ca6a66941a4032499f56aec8/2bb6315cb1db00c907bda1e2ae7a081c/icon-vertical-ellipsis.png?fm=webp&h=15)at the top of the page.
  5. Click **Delete**.
  6. If you are sure you want to delete the RFI, click **Delete**.


## See Also
  * [What is the RFI Manager role?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-the-rfi-manager-role)
  * [Retrieve an RFI from the Recycle Bin](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/retrieve-an-rfi-from-the-recycle-bin)
  * [Delete a Response to an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/delete-a-response-to-an-rfi)
title: Delete an RFI
description: How to delete an RFI from the RFIs tab.
